name: Girdle of Hippolyta
description: The Girdle of Hippolyta, also known as the Zoster of Hippolyta, was a magnificent belt worn by Hippolyta, Queen of the Amazons. It was a gift from her father, Ares, the god of war, and symbolized her authority and martial prowess. The girdle was not merely decorative; it possessed magical properties, granting Hippolyta immense strength and invincibility. The ninth labor of Heracles involved retrieving this girdle for Admete, the daughter of King Eurystheus. Heracles, accompanied by a band of heroes, sailed to the land of the Amazons. Accounts vary regarding how Heracles obtained the girdle. Some versions depict Hippolyta willingly offering the girdle as a token of her admiration for Heracles' bravery and strength. However, other accounts describe Hera, disguised as an Amazon, spreading rumors among the Amazons that Heracles intended to kidnap Hippolyta. This led to a fierce battle between Heracles and the Amazons, during which Heracles killed Hippolyta and seized the girdle. Regardless of the version, the Girdle of Hippolyta became a symbol of Heracles' triumph and a testament to the power of the Amazons. The girdle represented not only physical strength but also the authority and leadership of the Amazon queen, making it a highly coveted and dangerous artifact.
subtitle: Amazon Queen's Belt of Power

keyMyths.title: Heracles' Ninth Labor
keyMyths.description: The retrieval of the Girdle of Hippolyta was Heracles' ninth labor, assigned by King Eurystheus. The girdle was sought for Eurystheus' daughter, Admete, and proved to be a challenging and dangerous task for the hero.

keyMyths.title: Hippolyta's Gift or Battle
keyMyths.description: Accounts differ on how Heracles obtained the girdle; some say Hippolyta willingly gave it, while others claim he fought and killed her to take it. These varying narratives highlight the complex and often contradictory nature of Greek myths.

sources.author: Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology and heroic legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive account of Greek mythology and heroic legends.

sources.author: Diodorus Siculus
sources.description: A universal history covering various cultures, including Greek mythology.
